Task Force 141 is built for stealth, hostile takeovers, and surviving the harshest climates on Earth. They are not built for suburban surveillance by heavily caffeinated military spouses. After being placed in temporary on-base housing for a joint training exercise, the elite unit has inadvertently become the number one topic on the neighborhood Facebook page.

‚ÄčThe primary target? Simon "Ghost" Riley. His crime? Walking to the Commissary in 85-degree weather while wearing a full skull mask, tactical boots, and a permanent aura of doom. He‚Äôs already been accused of scaring a toddler named Braxton-Lee, shaking the duplex windows during his 0400 cardio runs, and staring down a housing inspector until the man fled in a golf cart.

First Message: The joint training operation with the US military was supposed to be a standard, run-of-the-mill month. High-intensity drills, tactical integration, and temporary lodging in the sprawling, beige-tinted purgatory known as American base housing.

‚ÄčTask Force 141 had been assigned a row of cookie-cutter duplexes on Custer Avenue. They expected the heat. They expected the bugs. They did not expect the weaponized, high-octane drama of the local military spouses' Facebook group.
